course_description.overview: Scenic road race starting at Knysna High School Sports Fields, along the N2 through Garden Route landscapes, featuring flat sections, Kyters Nek climb, Groen Vlei Lake, and for 100km, Serpentine climb to Hoekwil with forest, lagoon, and farmland views.

key_segments.name: Kyters Nek Climb
key_segments.description: Moderate 2.5km climb shortly after leaving Knysna, encountered twice in 50km route.

key_segments.name: Groen Vlei Lake
key_segments.description: Flat and scenic stretch past the lake towards Sedgefield.

key_segments.name: Serpentine Climb
key_segments.description: Challenging climb up to Hoekwil for 100km riders, with panoramic views.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Moderate for 50km with rolling terrain and two climbs; challenging for 100km due to extended distance, additional climbs, and undulating N2 return.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into George Airport (60km east), then drive N2 to Knysna; event during school holidays with good road access.
logistics.accommodation: Abundant options in Knysna including B&Bs, self-catering apartments, lodges near lagoon and forest; book early for Oyster Festival period.
logistics.registration_details: Online via official site; registration tent Friday 09:00-18:00, pickup Saturday 06:00-08:00; part of Knysna Oyster Festival; discounts for multi-event entries.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bike required; e-bikes welcome; start batches by category; traffic-controlled with lead vehicles.
logistics.local_considerations: Winter event (cool, possibly wet); family-friendly with MTB/crit options; Rotary Club organized; confirm dates with organizer.

final_verdict.one_liner: Stunning Garden Route road race blending coastal forests, climbs, and festival vibes.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Road cyclists seeking scenic challenge, from intermediates (50km) to experienced endurance riders (100km), e-bike users, families during Oyster Festival.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for breathtaking scenery and well-organized regional event value.
pro_tips: Train for Kyters Nek and Serpentine climbs.
Book accommodation months ahead for festival crowds.
Pack layers for cool July weather.
